summary: Initial C matching work for sysdolphin baselib AXDriver moved AXDriverAlloc and the no-op AXDriverFree out of asm/sysdolphin/baselib/AXDriver.s into a new src/sysdolphin/baselib/axdriver.c, added a matching header, and inserted the new C object before the remaining AXDriver assembly object in obj_files.mk. AXDriverAlloc was reconstructed as a bump allocator over AXDriver_804D77D4 using axfxallocsize and axfxmaxsize, preserving the original assert call on overflow.
what_changed: Deleted the handwritten assembly bodies for AXDriverAlloc and AXDriverFree from asm/sysdolphin/baselib/AXDriver.s, leaving later AXDriver assembly such as AXDriverUnlink in place.
Added src/sysdolphin/baselib/axdriver.c with C implementations of AXDriverAlloc and AXDriverFree.

AXDriverAlloc now computes a return pointer from AXDriver_804D77D4 plus the current axfxallocsize, increments axfxallocsize by the requested size, checks axfxallocsize against axfxmaxsize, and calls __assert with AXDriver_80408140, line 78, and AXDriver_8040814C if the allocation exceeds or reaches the max.
AXDriverFree was matched as an empty function taking a void* argument, corresponding to the original single blr assembly body.
Added src/sysdolphin/baselib/axdriver.h declaring AXDriverAlloc and AXDriverFree and including platform.h for fixed-width project types.
Updated obj_files.mk so $(BUILD_DIR)/src/sysdolphin/baselib/axdriver.c.o is linked immediately before $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/sysdolphin/baselib/AXDriver.s.o.
smart_moves: Kept the new C object before the residual AXDriver.s object in TEXT_O_FILES, preserving link order while only replacing the functions that were matched.
Used extern declarations for existing small-data and rodata symbols rather than prematurely naming or restructuring unknown globals.
Matched the assert path using the same file-string symbol, line number 78, and assertion-string symbol visible in the original assembly.
Represented AXDriverFree as an intentionally empty C function, which is the minimal source-level equivalent of the original blr-only assembly.
Limited the decompilation scope to AXDriverAlloc and AXDriverFree, avoiding disturbance to unmatched AXDriverUnlink and later assembly in the same file.
decomp_lessons: For partial file decompilation, remove only the matched symbols from the assembly file and add the C object before the remaining assembly object to preserve symbol layout and link behavior.
A bump allocator pattern in PowerPC assembly can often be expressed as taking a base pointer plus current offset, then updating the offset, then checking the new offset against a maximum.
When matching assert-using code, preserve the exact line number and string-symbol arguments; these constants are part of matching the generated code.
A function that compiles to only blr can be represented as an empty void function even if it accepts an unused pointer parameter.
Unknown data symbols can remain as address-based placeholder names in early matching work when there is insufficient evidence for better names.
naming_conventions: New source and header files use lowercase baselib naming: axdriver.c and axdriver.h, while the legacy assembly file remains AXDriver.s.
Public functions retain the existing AXDriver-prefixed camel-case names: AXDriverAlloc and AXDriverFree.
Unknown or not-yet-renamed data symbols retain address-style names such as AXDriver_804D77D4, AXDriver_80408140, and AXDriver_8040814C.
Global allocator state names axfxallocsize and axfxmaxsize are kept as existing lowercase symbols.
The header uses the project-style include guard _AXDRIVER_H_.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Translated lwz from axfxallocsize and AXDriver_804D77D4 plus add into pointer computation: &AXDriver_804D77D4[axfxallocsize].
Translated the assembly's update of axfxallocsize before the bounds check into axfxallocsize += size followed by if (axfxallocsize >= axfxmaxsize).
Matched the original cmplw/blt logic by asserting when the new allocation size is not below the max, expressed in C as >=.
Preserved the __assert call signature inferred from registers: file/string symbol AXDriver_80408140, line 78, assertion symbol AXDriver_8040814C.
Matched the return of saved r31 by returning the pre-increment allocation pointer.
Matched AXDriverFree's blr-only body with an empty function body.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename AXDriver_804D77D4, AXDriver_80408140, or AXDriver_8040814C.
Find later PRs that decompile AXDriverUnlink or the remaining asm/sysdolphin/baselib/AXDriver.s functions.
Search for additional baselib allocator matches using axfxallocsize and axfxmaxsize.
Check whether axdriver.h later gains more AXDriver declarations or struct definitions.
